MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Molly A. Friend of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party The W.W. Henry Company, LP

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE W.W. HENRY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Counsel for defendant did not inform the Court and the opposing party of her intention to contest the tentative ruling before the designated time on July 25, 2017. The Court did not allow counsel for defendant to argue and adopted the tentative ruling issued on July 25, 2017: Defendant The W.W. Henry Company, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Walter Zabel was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Larry Lay and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Additionally, the limited questioning of Mr. Lay fails to demonstrate that he does not know and cannot remember the number designation for the Henry's wet patch product at issue. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The declaration of Lori Angelo is insufficient to demonstrate that the Henry's wet patch product at issue was more likely than not a non-asbestos-containing product. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Lori Angelo are overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)
